title: Inference, Annotations, Literals, and Widening · Type Recall
summary: Model service categories, thresholds, identifiers, and configuration without widening away domain meaning. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a literal-safe service configuration model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict best-common, contextual, return, and evolving inference from the surrounding expression and use site.
Add annotations at public, recursive, overloaded, and external boundaries without redundantly annotating obvious locals.
Control literal widening with const bindings, readonly context, const assertions, and const type parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
